questions.question: A rectangular prism has length 5 cm, width 3 cm, and height 4 cm. What is its volume in cubic centimeters?
questions.explanation: Volume of a rectangular prism = length × width × height. V = 5 × 3 × 4 = 60 cm³.

questions.question: A cylinder has radius 2 inches and height 10 inches. What is its volume in terms of π?
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = πr²h. V = π(2)²(10) = π(4)(10) = 40π in³.

questions.question: A sphere has radius 3 meters. What is its volume in terms of π?
questions.explanation: Volume of a sphere = (4/3)πr³. V = (4/3)π(3)³ = (4/3)π(27) = 36π m³.

questions.question: A cone has radius 5 cm and height 12 cm. What is its volume in terms of π?
Two identical cubes each have side length 2 units. If you stack them to form a rectangular prism, what is the total volume?
questions.explanation: Each cube has volume 2³ = 8 cubic units. Two cubes together have volume 8 + 8 = 16 cubic units.

questions.question: A triangular prism has a triangular base with area 6 square inches and a height (depth) of 8 inches. What is the volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a prism = base area × height. V = 6 × 8 = 48 in³.
